questions.question: What is a frame of reference?
choices: A coordinate system from which motion is observed
questions.explanation: A frame of reference is a coordinate system from which motion is observed and measured.

questions.question: If a train moves east at 30 m/s and a passenger walks west at 2 m/s relative to the train, what is the passenger's velocity relative to the ground?
questions.explanation: Relative to the ground: 30 m/s east - 2 m/s west = 28 m/s east.

questions.question: Which equation gives the velocity of A relative to B?
A boat moves north at 5 m/s relative to the water. The river flows east at 3 m/s. What is the boat's speed relative to the ground?
If two cars move in the same direction, one at 60 km/h and the other at 80 km/h, what is their relative speed?
Why is relative motion important in physics?
choices: It helps describe motion from different perspectives
It is only used in astronomy
It only applies to stationary objects
questions.explanation: Relative motion allows us to describe and analyze motion from different frames of reference.
